name: linear
description: Manages Linear issues, teams, and projects via CLI. Lists issues, creates tasks, views details, links issues, and runs GraphQL queries. Must use for "my Linear issues", "create Linear task", "link issues in Linear", "Linear API query", or any Linear project management request.
capabilities: List and search issues across teams with filters
Create, view, update, and delete issues
Link related issues and manage parent-child relationships
Add comments to issues from command line or file
Run raw GraphQL queries against Linear API
List teams, projects, and manage project membership
author: 0xBigBoss
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown files with no executable code. Provides instructions for using the external @0xbigboss/linear-cli Zig binary. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - backticks are markdown code formatting, temp directory paths are example file paths for GraphQL queries, hardcoded URLs are documentation links, and environment variable references are legitimate auth configuration guidance.
valueStatement: Track and manage Linear issues without leaving your terminal. Create tasks, link issues, view details, and run GraphQL queries. Automate your project management workflow with a simple CLI interface.
userTitle: Manage Linear issues and projects from command line
limitations: Requires separate installation of @0xbigboss/linear-cli Zig binary
File upload requires multi-step GraphQL process
Parent issue linking requires UUIDs, not issue identifiers
Interactive confirmation required for mutations without --yes flag

useCases.title: Track development tasks
useCases.description: Create issues, assign work, and link related tasks directly from your terminal during coding sessions.

useCases.title: Monitor team progress
useCases.description: List issues by team, filter by assignee, and query project status without opening a browser.

useCases.title: Automate issue workflows
useCases.description: Integrate Linear operations into CI/CD pipelines and automated workflows via CLI or GraphQL.

promptTemplates.title: List my issues
promptTemplates.prompt: List my open issues in the engineering team

promptTemplates.title: Create a task
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue in the engineering team titled Fix login bug with high priority

promptTemplates.title: Link related issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Link issue ENG-123 as blocking ENG-456

promptTemplates.title: Run GraphQL query
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all workflow states for the engineering team using GraphQL

bestPractices: Use --yes flag for all mutations to avoid interactive prompts in scripts
Get issue UUIDs with --json output when using --parent flag
Set default team and output format with linear config set commands
antiPatterns: Using issue identifiers where UUIDs are required (--parent flag)
Running mutations without --yes in automated scripts
Hard-coding team IDs instead of using team keys

faq.question: How do I install the Linear CLI?
faq.answer: Run npm install -g @0xbigboss/linear-cli. Requires Zig 0.15.2 to build from source.

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: Create a personal API key at linear.app/settings/api. Set it with linear auth set or LINEAR_API_KEY env var.

faq.question: Can I use this with any Linear workspace?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI works with any Linear workspace where your API key has appropriate permissions.

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: Auth uses Authorization header (no Bearer). Precedence: CLI flags, then LINEAR_API_KEY env, then config file.

faq.question: Why am I getting empty results?
faq.answer: Most likely missing --team flag. Check default config or specify --team TEAM_KEY explicitly.

faq.question: How is this different from the Linear API directly?
faq.answer: The CLI wraps the GraphQL API with convenient commands, filtering, and JSON formatting. Use linear gql for raw queries.
